usage: add_target_pg [-help] [-area {x1 y1 x2 y2}{x1 y1 x2 y2 x3 y3 x4 y4 ...}] [-force] [-instance {list_of_instances}] -nets {list_of_nets} -pin_layer <layer> [-shape {STRIPE IOWIRE COREWIRE BLOCKWIRE}] [-stack_layer_config {stack_layer_pattern}] [-stack_layer_config_file <filename>] -target_layer <layer> [-uda <subclass_string>]

options.name: -area
options.description: {x1 y1 x2 y2}{x1 y1 x2 y2 x3 y3 x4 y4 ...} The instance inside the specified area can be connected

options.name: -force
options.description: Tool will keep the added PG even the connection has DRC, and leaves there a DRC marker

options.name: -instance
options.description: Specify the name of instances to be connected

options.name: -nets
options.description: Specify the power/ground net names to be connected

options.name: -pin_layer
options.description: Specify the layer name of the cell to be connected

options.name: -shape
options.description: {STRIPE IOWIRE COREWIRE BLOCKWIRE} Specifies the wires and vias shape

options.name: -stack_layer_config
options.description: {stack_layer_pattern} The value of this option defines the pattern of the stripes on intermediate layers between pin layer and target layer

options.name: -stack_layer_config_file
options.description: A config file defining the pattern of the stripes on intermediate layer between pin layer and target layer

options.name: -target_layer
options.description: Specify the layer name of the destination power/ground stripe

options.name: -uda
options.description: Indicate the user defined attribute subcalss of wires to route
